title: “I use Thunderbird on my PC with Outlook on UVM servers. After a password change, I can receive but not send email. What do I need to fix? I’d prefer not to switch to Outlook as my interface. Thanks. –Turner”
creator: osler.turner
content:
I am an emeritus professor using the UVM email account X@uvm.edu<mailto:X@uvm.edu>, which I have used for many years. I use Mozilla Thunderbird (current version, “Eclipse”) as my email client and my account is configured for UVM’s Microsoft 365/Exchange Online service. After my most recent required yearly password change, although I can still receive email in Thunderbird, I can no longer send email. When I attempt to send a message, I consistently see this error: “Login to account ‘smtp.office365.com’ failed. Login to server smtp.office365.com with username tosler@uvm.edu<mailto:X@uvm.edu> failed.” Here are my current settings: 

 *

Incoming (IMAP): 

    *

Server: outlook.office365.com 

    *

Port: 993 

    *

Connection security: SSL/TLS 

    *

Authentication method: OAuth2 

    *

Username: tosler@uvm.edu 

 *

Outgoing (SMTP): 

    *

Server: smtp.office365.com (I have also tested smtp.uvm.edu with the same result) 

    *

Port: 587 

    *

Connection security: STARTTLS 

    *

Authentication method: OAuth2 

    *

Username: X@uvm.edu

I have reinstalled Thunderbird, and I have removed all saved passwords and tokens for outlook.office365.com and smtp.office365.com, restarted the program, and completed the Microsoft/UVM sign‑in prompt again. IMAP continues to work and mail arrives normally, but SMTP authentication still fails with the error above. Because: 

 *

The configuration matches Microsoft 365/Exchange Online client‑submission settings, and 

 *

IMAP with OAuth2 works, while SMTP with OAuth2 fails,

it appears that this may be a server‑side policy or mailbox setting, rather than a client configuration problem. What are my options? Does the University of Vermont have to change some setting on their end on my behalf? Thanks. -turner

(PII redacted by moderator)

title: Calendar event reminders not showing before event
creator: ronald8
content:
I'm long time user of Thunderbird Calendar. For most of the events in calendar I have reminders that should pop up for example 7 days before the event. This was working correctly.

About half year ago. The reminders stopped working. I don't get the reminder 7 days before, but they pop up on the day of the event. This is quite unpleasant. For example If I have my car technical check deadline and I get the reminder on the deadline, not 7 days ahead.

I have not changed any settings around the time it started happening.

So far I have checked: -Individual event reminder settings : yes they still got the reminder set up to pop up some days before the event -Settings / Calendar / Reminders / Show the reminder dialog - checked -Settings / Calendar / Notification / Show notifications for upcoming events - I added here "1 day before event starts" notification, but that also doesn't work. -Tried Googling the issue. Found some threads about reminders not popping at all. Not the case here. I don't get notifications before the event, but they pop up at the time of the event. No luck here.

Since about half a year has passed since this started, I guess it's not a bug that happens to everyone.

title: How i can remove duplicate emails from thunderbird files?
creator: Techinmay
content: Duplicate emails in Mozilla Thunderbird can occur due to repeated imports, sync issues, or backup restores, and removing them manually is time-consuming and unreliable. While Thunderbird has limited built-in options and add-ons, they often miss complex duplicates. A more efficient and safe approach is to use an automated solution like SysTools MBOX Duplicate Remover. It scans Thunderbird MBOX files thoroughly and removes duplicates based on multiple criteria such as To, From, Subject, Date, and attachments. The tool also preserves folder structure and ensures no data loss, making it a dependable way to clean up Thunderbird files quickly and accurately.

title: Sending of the message failed.
creator: marc.defossez
content:
Hi,

OS: Linux Manjaro 6.18.12-1 (Cinnamon 6.6.7) Thunderbird: 147.0.1 Email: google account

Everything worked perfect till I upgraded to version 147.0.1 When after replying or writing new email, hitting the [send] button I always get this error message

"Sending of the message failed. The message could not be sent because the connection to Outgoing server (SMTP) smtp.gmail.com timed out. Try again."

Click [OK] and save the message or messages is dropping them in the 'Drafts' folder. Close Thunderbird and start it again. Then double click the messages in the [Drafts] folder and hit the [Send] button, will send the messages without problem.

Nothing changed to my setup, I only upgraded to 147.0.1. Another weird thing: every-time I start Thunderbird it creates a Thunderbird directory under my home.

Is one connected to the other? Has somebody an idea what's going on/wrong?

Many thanks in advance
